notes: Esync disabled, even after raising the file limit as described in the FAQ. After 44 hours of play: ran mostly without issues with the exception of 2 CTD's due to renderer issue. 50 - 100 fps.
title: Phantom Doctrine

notes: Mostly playable. Strategic map is usually glitching (cosmetic only: map looks like an extreme motion blur is applied to it, but markers are visible; using PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 fixes this issue but adds shading problems and kills framerate). Sometimes mission result popups stop appearing (workaround: save and reload) or some or all keyboard shortcuts stop working after initial tactical mission loading screen (requires restart).
title: Phantom Doctrine

notes: Updating from Bronze to Gold after finding solution to issues mentioned below. Most of them, apparently, happened when the game tried to show a story/loading movie: when finishing a story mission or moving hideout. Requires a WMF fix (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/838, look for wmf_workaround.zip) AND mfplay.dll from Win7 SP1. After that seems to run perfectly.
title: Phantom Doctrine

notes:
Install xact via winetricks / protontricks to fix the broken sound.

The world map is a blurry mess. It doesn't affect gameplay much but it's painful to look at. Because of this it is a Silver and not a Gold.
title: Phantom Doctrine

notes: Load old save for 5 minutes - it works fine. Started new game. First time game crashes when I was changing char name. Started new game again with new game. First mission is fine except minor freezes, got crashed in middle of second misson
title: Phantom Doctrine
